text: Three fair coins are tossed simultaneously. Find \(P(X^{-1}(2))\), where \(X\) counts the number of heads.
explanation: \(X^{-1}(2)\) is the event of getting exactly 2 heads in 3 tosses. \(P(X=2)={}^3C_2\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^3=\dfrac{3}{8}\).
name: Probability of 2 Heads in 3 Tosses

text: A bag contains 3 red, 5 blue and 8 green balls. A ball is selected at random. The probability of NOT getting a blue ball is
explanation: Total balls \(=16\). Blue balls \(=5\), so non-blue \(=11\). \(P(\text{not blue})=\dfrac{11}{16}\).
name: Complementary Probability

text: Suppose \(A\) and \(B\) are two events such that \(P(A)=0.9\), \(P(B)=0.8\) and \(P(A\cap B)\geq0.7\). Then the case \(P(A\cup B)\leq1\) is
explanation: Maximum \(P(A\cup B)=P(A)+P(B)-P(A\cap B)\leq0.9+0.8-0.7=1\). Since \(P(A\cup B)\leq1\) always holds (probability cannot exceed 1), and here the maximum equals exactly 1, the condition is always true.
name: Probability Inequality

text: A die is rolled three times. The probability of getting a sum equal to a prime of the form \(4n+1\) is
explanation: Primes of form \(4n+1\) in [3,18]: 5, 13, 17. Count: sum\(=5\): 6 ways; sum\(=13\): 21 ways; sum\(=17\): 3 ways. Total favourable \(=30\). \(P=\dfrac{30}{216}=\dfrac{5}{36}\).
name: Sum is Prime of Form 4n+1

text: A box contains 4 defective and 6 good machines. Two machines are selected without replacement. The probability that both are good is
name: Probability Both Machines are Good

text: Three boxes contain balls of different colours. Box 1: 1 black, 2 white, 3 red. Box 2: 1 black, 1 white, 2 red. Box 3: 5 black, 4 white, 1 red. A box is chosen at random, 3 balls drawn (all different colours). Find the probability they came from Box 2.
name: Bayes Theorem — Three Boxes

text: Die A has 4 red and 2 white faces; Die B has 2 red and 4 white faces. A coin is tossed: heads → Die A, tails → Die B. If the probability that Die A was used given red appears every time in \(n\) throws is \(\dfrac{32}{33}\), then \(n =\)
name: Bayes Theorem — Dice and Coin

text: In a game, a person wins ₹5 for rolling >4, loses ₹1 otherwise on a fair die, and quits when he gets >4. The expected amount he wins/loses is
name: Expected Value in a Sequential Game

text: The binomial distribution with mean 9 and standard deviation \(\dfrac{3}{2}\) is equal to
explanation: \(np=9\) and \(\sqrt{npq}=\tfrac{3}{2}\Rightarrow npq=\tfrac{9}{4}\). So \(q=\tfrac{npq}{np}=\tfrac{9/4}{9}=\tfrac{1}{4}\) and \(p=\tfrac{3}{4}\). Then \(n=\tfrac{9}{3/4}=12\). Distribution: \(\left(\tfrac{3}{4}+\tfrac{1}{4}\right)^{12}\).
name: Identifying Binomial Distribution

text: Three squares of a chessboard are selected at random. The probability of selecting 2 squares of one colour and 1 of a different colour is
name: Chessboard Squares Colour Probability

text: When a coin is tossed 6 times, the probability of getting more heads than tails is
explanation: More heads than tails means \(\geq4\) heads. \(P(X=4)+P(X=5)+P(X=6)=\dfrac{{}^6C_4+{}^6C_5+{}^6C_6}{2^6}=\dfrac{15+6+1}{64}=\dfrac{22}{64}=\dfrac{11}{32}\).
name: More Heads than Tails in 6 Tosses

text: P and Q each toss 3 coins. The probability that both get the same number of heads is
name: Same Number of Heads for Two Players

text: A and B are two candidates seeking admission. \(P(A)=0.7\) and \(P(\text{exactly one selected})=0.6\). The probability that B is selected is
name: Probability B Selected (Exactly One)

text: The probability for A to fail in an exam is 0.2 and for B is 0.3. The probability that either A or B fails is \(\leq\)
name: Union Probability Upper Bound

text: Bill and George shoot at a target. Bill hits with probability 0.7, George independently with 0.4. Given exactly one shot hit, the probability it was George's is
name: Conditional Probability — One Shot Hit

text: For any two events \(A\) and \(B\), if \(P(A\cup B)=aP(A\cap B)+bP(A)+cP(B)\), then \(3a+2b+5c =\)
name: Identifying Coefficients in P(A∪B)

text: In 3 binomial trials, \(P(\text{2 successes})=9\cdot P(\text{3 successes})\). The probability of success in each trial is
name: Binomial Success Probability from Ratio

text: A random variable \(X\) has PMF with \(K=\dfrac{1}{8}\) and \(P(X=k)\) given for \(k=1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8\). If \(E=\{X\text{ is prime}\}\) and \(F=\{X<4\}\), then \(P(E\cup F)=\)
name: P(E∪F) from PMF

text: Three unbiased dice are thrown and show different faces. Given this, the probability their total is 8 is
explanation: All different faces: \({}^6C_3=20\) combinations. Totalling 8 with all different: \((1,2,5)\) and \((1,3,4)\) — only 2 combinations. \(P=\dfrac{2}{20}=\dfrac{1}{10}\).
name: Conditional Probability — Sum=8, All Different

text: Five different books are distributed among four students randomly. The probability that each student gets at least one book is
explanation: Total ways \(=4^5=1024\). By inclusion-exclusion, ways where each gets \(\geq1\) book (surjective distributions) \(=\dfrac{5!}{1!1!1!2!}\times4!=240\). \(P=\dfrac{240}{1024}=\dfrac{15}{64}\).
name: Surjective Distribution Probability

text: A 2×2 determinant is chosen at random with elements 0 or 1. The probability it is non-zero is
explanation: Total matrices \(=2^4=16\). Non-zero determinant matrices: \(\begin{pmatrix}1&0\\0&1\end{pmatrix}, \begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}, \begin{pmatrix}1&1\\0&1\end{pmatrix}, \begin{pmatrix}1&0\\1&1\end{pmatrix}, \begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&1\end{pmatrix}, \begin{pmatrix}1&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}\) — 6 matrices. \(P=\dfrac{6}{16}=\dfrac{3}{8}\).
name: Non-Zero 2×2 Determinant Probability

text: Three books are drawn with replacement from books numbered 1 to 20. The probability that the largest number is 7 is
name: Largest Number in Sampling with Replacement

text: When six coins are tossed simultaneously, the probability of getting at least 4 heads is
name: At Least 4 Heads in 6 Coin Tosses

text: The probability of a non-leap year having 53 Mondays is
explanation: A non-leap year has 365 days \(=52\) weeks \(+1\) day. The extra day can be any of 7 days. For 53 Mondays, the extra day must be Monday: probability \(=\dfrac{1}{7}\).
name: 53 Mondays in a Non-Leap Year

text: A bag has 4 red and 6 blue pens. 3 pens are drawn one after another. Probability all 3 are blue is
name: All 3 Drawn are Blue

text: Three dice are thrown. Given their sum is 8, the probability one of them shows 4 is
name: Conditional Probability — Sum=8, One Die=4

text: Accidents on a highway follow Poisson distribution with parameter 3. The probability no accidents occur today is
name: Poisson Distribution — Zero Events

text: 75 students are split into sections of 30 and 45. The probability that two particular students are always in the same section is
name: Two Students in Same Section

text: A bag contains \(2n\) coins; \((n-1)\) are unfair (heads on both sides), rest are fair. One coin is picked and tossed. \(P(\text{head})=\dfrac{41}{56}\). Number of unfair coins is
name: Unfair Coins in Bag

text: Bag A: 6 green, 8 red. Bag B: 9 green, 5 red. A card from a deck is drawn — if spade, draw from A; otherwise from B. Two balls drawn are same colour. The probability they came from A is
name: Bayes Theorem — Bag Selection

text: For Poisson distribution with unit mean, \(\displaystyle\sum_{x=0}^{\infty}|x-\bar{x}|P(X=x)\) (where \(\bar{x}=1\)) equals
name: Mean Absolute Deviation in Poisson Distribution

text: Two boxes A and B each contain chits numbered 1 to 10. One chit from each is drawn. The probability that the number from A is smaller than from B is
name: Probability A < B from Two Sets

text: An envelope is from 'LONDON' or 'CLIFTON'. Two successive letters 'ON' are visible. The probability the envelope is from LONDON is
explanation: LONDON has 5 pairs of consecutive letters: (LO,ON,ND,DO,ON) — 'ON' appears 2 times. CLIFTON has 6 pairs: 'ON' appears 1 time. \(P(\text{London}|\text{ON})=\dfrac{\frac{1}{2}\cdot\frac{2}{5}}{\frac{1}{2}\cdot\frac{2}{5}+\frac{1}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{6}}=\dfrac{12}{12+5}=\dfrac{12}{17}\).
name: Bayes Theorem — Envelope Word

text: A PMF is given by \(P(X=k)=ck^2\) for \(k\in\{0,1,2,3,4\}\). Then \(\sigma^2+\mu^2=\)
name: Variance and Mean from PMF

text: In a book of 250 pages there are 200 typographical errors. Assuming Poisson distribution, the probability a random sample of 5 pages contains no error is
explanation: Error rate \(\lambda=\dfrac{200}{250}=\dfrac{4}{5}\) per page. For 5 pages: \(\lambda'=5\cdot\dfrac{4}{5}=4\). \(P(X=0)=e^{-4}\).
name: Poisson — No Errors in 5 Pages

text: A and B each select one number at random from \(\{1,2,3,\ldots,n\}\). \(P(A<B)=\dfrac{1009}{2019}\). The probability that B's number is immediately next to A's is
explanation: \(P(A<B)=\dfrac{n-1}{2n}=\dfrac{1009}{2019}\Rightarrow2019(n-1)=2018n\Rightarrow n=2019\). Pairs where B is immediately next to A: 2018 (consecutive pairs). \(P=\dfrac{2018}{2019^2}\).
name: Consecutive Number Selection

text: Three bags A, B, C contain balls: A has 2W,3B; B has 4W,2B; C has 3W,2B. A ball drawn from a randomly chosen bag is black. The probability it is from B is
name: Bayes Theorem — Black Ball

text: A box has 4 white and 5 red balls. 3 are drawn randomly. The mean number of red balls drawn is
name: Mean of Hypergeometric Distribution

text: If \(P(A)=0.3,\ P(B)=0.4,\ P(\bar{A}\cap B)=0.5\), then \(P(A\cup B)+P(B|A\cup B)=\)
explanation: \(P(A\cap\bar{B})=0.3-P(A\cap B)\). \(P(\bar{A}\cap B)=0.5\Rightarrow P(A\cap B)=P(B)-0.5=4-0.5=-0.1\)... more carefully: \(P(A\cup B)=0.3+0.4-P(A\cap B)=0.9\). \(P(B|A\cup B)=\dfrac{P(B)}{P(A\cup B)}=\dfrac{0.4\cdot\text{fraction}}{0.9}\)... PDF gives 1.15.
name: Combined Probability Expression

text: A speaks truth 4 out of 5 times. A die is tossed and reports a 4. The probability it was actually 4 is
explanation: \(P(E_1)=\tfrac{1}{6}\) (4 on die), \(P(A\text{ says 4}|E_1)=\tfrac{4}{5}\), \(P(A\text{ says 4}|E_2)=\tfrac{1}{5}\cdot\tfrac{1}{5}=\tfrac{1}{5}\) (lies and picks one of 5 others... or just \(P=\tfrac{1}{5}\)). By Bayes: \(P(E_1|\text{says 4})=\dfrac{\frac{1}{6}\cdot\frac{4}{5}}{\frac{1}{6}\cdot\frac{4}{5}+\frac{5}{6}\cdot\frac{1}{5}}=\dfrac{4/30}{4/30+1/6}=\dfrac{4/30}{9/30}=\dfrac{4}{9}\).
name: Bayes Theorem — Truth Teller

text: If \(X\) is a Poisson variate with \(P(X=2)=9P(X=4)+90P(X=6)\), then (Mean, Variance) of \(X\) is
name: Poisson Parameter from Probability Equation

text: From 50 students in two sections (20 and 30), the probability that Ram and Rahim are in the same section is
name: Two Students in Same Section

text: Two dice are rolled simultaneously. \(A\) = event of sum \(\geq8\), \(B\) = event of at least one die \(\leq3\). Then \(P(B|A) =\)
explanation: Event \(A\) has 15 outcomes. \(A\cap B\) (sum\(\geq8\) and at least one die\(\leq3\)): \((2,6),(3,5),(5,3),(6,2),(3,6),(6,3)\) — 6 outcomes. \(P(B|A)=\dfrac{6}{15}\).
name: Conditional Probability — Sum and Die Value

text: If \(P(X=0)=3C^3,\ P(X=2)=5C-10C^2,\ P(X=4)=4C-1\) and \(\sum P(X)=1\), then the variance of the distribution is
name: Variance from Given PMF

text: A box has 30 toys: 10 white, 20 blue. 5 toys drawn with replacement. Probability of at most 2 white toys is
name: At Most 2 White Toys (Binomial)

text: Two dice thrown simultaneously until sum is 7 or 11. Probability that 7 comes before 11 is
name: 7 Before 11 in Dice Throwing

text: If \(X\) is a Poisson variate with \(2P(X=1)=5P(X=5)+2P(X=3)\), the standard deviation of \(X\) is
name: Poisson SD from Probability Equation

text: The probability of occurrence of event \(A\) is \(\dfrac{2}{5}\) and non-occurrence of another independent event \(B\) is \(\dfrac{3}{10}\). The probability that only one of the two events occurs is
name: Probability Exactly One Event Occurs
